title: World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 (Fully Funded) — Call for Applications in South Korea
summary: Overview Applications are now open for the World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 , a fully funded international programme organized within the framework of the UNESCO World Heritage Education Programme . Hosted by the Republic of Korea through the Korea Heritage Service (KHS) and the Korean National Commission for UNESCO (KNCU), the forum will take place from 13 21 July 2026 alongside the 48th Session of the World Heritage Committee . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Under the theme World Heritage, Communities and Education: Empowering Youth as Agents of Change, the programme will bring together 30 young professionals from around the world to strengthen their knowledge, skills, and engagement in the protection and management of natural and cultural World Heritage sites. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Forum Theme and Objectives The 2026 forum focuses on the relationship between World Heritage, communities, and education, with the goal of empowering youth to play an active role in heritage conservation and governance. Key objectives include: Strengthening the commitment and professional capacity of young practitioners working in heritage-related fields Exploring how community participation can improve conservation and management practices Examining the role of education, research, and learning in informed decision-making Promoting youth leadership and inclusive governance in World Heritage protection Through exchanges with local and international experts, participants will engage in collaborative learning and develop practical perspectives on sustainable heritage management. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Programme Structure The forum combines academic discussions with practical, site-based learning experiences at World Heritage properties in the Republic of Korea. Activities include: Thematic presentations and expert-led discussions Collaborative workshops and group work Site visits and field-based learning Peer exchange with international participants Joint session with the World Heritage Site Managers Forum At the conclusion of the programme, participants will collectively prepare and present a Youth Declaration to the 48th Session of the World Heritage Committee. Provisional Schedule 12 July 2026: Arrival of participants 13 July 2026: Opening of the forum and introductions 14 19 July 2026: Thematic sessions, workshops, and site-based learning 18 July 2026: Joint session with the World Heritage Site Managers Forum 19 July 2026: Drafting of the Youth Declaration 20 July 2026: Finalisation and presentation of the Youth Declaration 21 July 2026: Closing session and departure Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Age Between 23 and 32 years old (born between 1994 and 2003 inclusive) Professional Profile Applicants should be young professionals or practitioners engaged in one or more of the following areas: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Education and teaching in formal learning settings World Heritage site management Research and innovation related to World Heritage NGOs or community-level associations focused on heritage Additional Requirements Demonstrated involvement in World Heritage-related activities Strong motivation to contribute to discussions on youth, communities, and education Full availability for the entire duration of the forum Proficiency in English (the working language of the programme) Selection Considerations The selection process aims to ensure: Balanced geographical representation Gender equality Diversity of professional backgrounds Potential for knowledge-sharing and impact after the forum Funding and Benefits The World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 is fully funded. Selected participants will receive: International economy-class travel Accommodation Local transportation Meals during the programme Visa support and related fees where applicable Participants wishing to extend their stay after the programme must cover additional expenses independently. Application Process Interested candidates must apply through the official online application form: Application Link: https://unesco-2023.limesurvey.net/662537 Application Deadline: 15 March 2026 (23:59 CET) Important notes: Only complete applications submitted through the online form will be considered Selection is based solely on the information provided in the application Recommendations or endorsements are not required Selected applicants will be notified via email. Contact Information For questions regarding the application process: Email: For any questions, kindly contact: wh-ypf@unesco.org Why You Should Apply The World Heritage Young Professionals Forum is a flagship initiative of UNESCO s World Heritage Education Programme. It offers young professionals a unique opportunity to: Build international networks with peers and experts Gain practical experience through site-based learning Deepen understanding of the World Heritage Convention Contribute directly to global discussions on heritage conservation Strengthen leadership skills in community-based and sustainable heritage management For professionals working at the intersection of heritage, education, and community engagement, the forum provides both international exposure and long-term professional value. Call for Applications Selection of participants will be carried out solely on the basis of the online applications, in accordance with the criteria specified in the Call for Applications. Recommendations, nominations, or endorsements will therefore not be considered. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Online application Applications must be submitted through the online application form by 15 March 2026 at 23:59 CET Application Link: https://unesco-2023.limesurvey.net/662537 For any questions, kindly contact: wh-ypf@unesco.org For more info please visit: https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/2862/ The post World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 (Fully Funded) — Call for Applications in South Korea appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: George C. Marshall Fellowship 2026: Develop Grand Strategy Skills and Advance U.S. National Security Leadership
summary: Introduction: A Premier Fellowship in Grand Strategy and Strategic Leadership The George C. Marshall Fellowship is a prestigious leadership development program offered by The Heritage Foundation . Designed for exceptional conservative professionals early in their careers, this fellowship provides a comprehensive foundation in grand strategy theory and practice , strategic leadership, and the integration of national power instruments to advance U.S. national security interests. Inspired by the legacy of George C. Marshall , the program equips fellows with the intellectual framework and practical tools needed to address complex global challenges while preserving the principles of free enterprise, limited government, individual freedom, traditional American values, and a strong national defense. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The 2025 George C. Marshall Fellowship is structured as an immersive, multi-month experience combining expert-led discussions, hands-on workshops, historical analysis, and real-world strategic exercises. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the George C. Marshall Fellowship Mission The mission of the George C. Marshall Fellows program is to provide exceptional conservative professionals with the opportunity to: Apply the principles of grand strategy. Integrate all instruments of national power (diplomatic, informational, military, and economic). Develop practical approaches to achieving U.S. national security objectives. Strengthen leadership capabilities grounded in conservative policy principles. Vision The program offers a comprehensive overview of grand strategy theory and its practical application to today s global challenges. It is designed for individuals early in their careers who are likely to take on significant responsibilities in advancing U.S. national security. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants gain: Exposure to leading national security experts. Practical insights into defense and foreign policy. A structured understanding of geopolitical threats. Real-world exercises simulating strategic decision-making. The fellowship emphasizes both theoretical depth and applied leadership training , ensuring participants leave with actionable knowledge. 2026 George C. Marshall Fellowship Program Schedule The 2026 cohort follows a structured curriculum running from April through September. Program Launch April 30 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and discussion with a guest speaker. This opening session introduces fellows to the program s intellectual framework and sets expectations for the months ahead. Defense Budget 101 May 14 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Practical briefing and workshop on the Defense Budget. This session provides an in-depth understanding of: The U.S. defense budgeting process. Strategic resource allocation. Budget priorities and trade-offs. How funding decisions reflect national strategy. Participants engage in hands-on analysis to connect policy objectives with fiscal realities. Gettysburg Staff Ride May 29 (All Day) Day trip, lunch, and battlefield tour in Gettysburg, Pennsylvania. This immersive historical experience at Gettysburg Battlefield allows fellows to: Study strategic decision-making in historical context. Analyze leadership under pressure. Evaluate operational successes and failures. Draw modern lessons from Civil War strategy. Staff rides are a traditional military leadership development tool, reinforcing critical thinking and situational analysis. Module 2: Deterring China June 11 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and discussion with a guest speaker. June 25 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Practical exercise. This module focuses on: The strategic challenge posed by China. Deterrence theory and application. Military, economic, and diplomatic tools. Scenario-based strategic exercises. Fellows examine current geopolitical tensions and develop policy recommendations within a structured framework. Module 3: Hemispheric Security July 9 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and discussion with a guest speaker. July 23 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and panel discussion. This module addresses: Western Hemisphere security dynamics. U.S. relations with regional partners. Emerging threats in the Americas. Strategic coordination across institutions. Participants engage with policy experts and panelists to explore multidimensional security challenges. Program Graduation September 10 (Time and location TBD) Dinner and happy hour. The program concludes with a formal graduation event recognizing fellows achievements and fostering long-term professional networks. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Why Apply for the George C. Marshall Fellowship? The fellowship provides: Advanced training in grand strategy. Direct engagement with national security experts. Applied policy workshops. Leadership development grounded in conservative principles. Networking with peers committed to advancing U.S. national security. Exposure to real-world policy analysis and strategic planning. A prestigious credential associated with The Heritage Foundation. This is not merely a lecture series. It is an immersive strategic leadership development experience. Application Process Interested candidates are encouraged to apply through the official application portal. For more information click here Apply here Discove more fellowships opportunities here The post George C. Marshall Fellowship 2026: Develop Grand Strategy Skills and Advance U.S. National Security Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
